content: I'm trying to get my finances in order. At 57, I feel like I should have more saved up than I do.
It's never too late to start planning. What does your current income look like?
I make about $85,000 a year as a music producer. The DJ gigs bring in another $15-20,000 depending on the season.
That's a good combined income. What are your biggest expenses?
Mortgage on my studio space is $2,200 a month. Then there's my home brewing equipment - I just spent $600 on a new fermenter system.
Sounds like you have some expensive hobbies. How much do you spend on marathon running?
Entry fees are about $100-200 per race, and I do 4-5 marathons a year. Plus travel costs to races out of state.
That adds up quickly. Do you have any retirement savings currently?
About $45,000 in my 401(k). I started late though - didn't really contribute until I was 45.
It's great that you're thinking about this now. What are your goals for the next five years?
I want to pay off my studio mortgage, save at least $100,000 more for retirement, and maybe cut back on some expenses.
Those are achievable goals with some planning. Have you considered your volunteering at the animal shelter as a way to offset some costs?
Actually, that's a good point. I could cut back on my brewing supplies and use some of that time for more paid gigs instead.
